Good — clean slate. Now let me compile all this research into a comprehensive course artifact:
[Response Cleared]

I've created a comprehensive **WebGIS Mastery Course** based on thorough research across the current 2025–2026 landscape. Here's the high-level structure:

### What's Inside

| Module | Focus | Weeks |
|:---|:---|:---|
| **Module 1** | Web Mapping Foundations (Leaflet, OpenLayers) | 1–3 |
| **Module 2** | Spatial Databases & APIs (PostGIS, FastAPI) | 4–6 |
| **Module 3** | Map Servers & OGC Standards (GeoServer, pygeoapi) | 7–9 |
| **Module 4** | Modern Frontend & Visualization (MapLibre, React, deck.gl, Turf.js) | 10–12 |
| **Module 5** | Cloud-Native & Serverless (COG, PMTiles, STAC, GeoParquet) | 13–15 |
| **Module 6** | 3D, Real-Time & GeoAI (CesiumJS, WebSockets, ML integration) | 16–18 |
| **Module 7** | Security, DevOps & Production (Docker, CI/CD, performance) | 19–20 |
| **Module 8** | Capstone Projects & Portfolio Strategy | 21–24 |

### Key Highlights
- **6 hands-on projects** embedded throughout (one per module pair)
